samples.xml_description:
<module id="accu">
    <description>Implement a module to achieve serial input data accumulation output, input is 8bit data. The valid_in will be set to 1 before the first data comes in. Whenever the module receives 4 input data, the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results and sets the valid_out to be 1 (will last only 1 cycle).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="IDLE">0</parameter>
        <parameter name="ADD">1</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state" width_description="[1:0]">State register for the FSM.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="count" width_description="[1:0]">Counter register to count the number of input data.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="sum" width_description="[9:0]">Register to store the accumulated sum.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        State machine:
        - State IDLE: Wait for valid_in to be 1.
        - State ADD: Accumulate data_in and increment count until count reaches 3 (4 input data).
        Output:
        - When count reaches 3, set valid_out to 1 and output the accumulated sum in data_out.
        - When count is not 3, set valid_out to 0 and do not output anything.
    </implementation>
</module>
